## Author

## All Posts

- [Basic Syntax](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Basic-Syntax/) - This tutorial will learn about python interpreters and how to use them to do python programming and understand various functions and methods used in python interpreters.
- [Beginner's Guide to LangChain in Python](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Beginner-Guide-to-LangChain-in-Python/) - In this blog, we will learn about the LangChain framework, which is used to develop LLM applications that include ChatGPT with memory and long-term memory. These features will be explored in detail in future tutorials.
- [Python Data Types](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Data-Types/) - In all programming languages, data types are the essential concept. Variables use data types to store different types of data that do different things. Unlike other programming languages like C, C++, or JAVA, we do not need to declare data type keywords to declare a variable. In Python, we just need to declare the name of the variable and its value. 
- [First Python Program](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/First-Python-Program/) - In this tutorial, we will learn how to execute python code on python idle; the same steps can be followed using Windows, Linux, and Mac OS.
- [How to calculate coefficient of variation in Python](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/How-to-calculate-coefficient-of-variation-in-Python/) - In this tutorial, we will learn about the coefficient of variation, How to calculate coefficient of variation in python, and the importance of the coefficient of variation in artificial intelligence.
- [Installation of Python](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Installation-of-Python/) - In this part, we will learn to set up python on our computer, and once we do that, we will be ready to run our first python program.
- [Python Comments](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-Comments/) - This tutorial will learn about documenting and commenting on the python code using python comments and python docstring.
- [Python Keywords and Identifiers](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-Keywords-and-Identifiers/) - This tutorial will teach about keywords and identifiers, which will include reserved words in python.
- [Python Type Casting or Type Conversion](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-Type-Conversion-and-Type-Casting/) - In this tutorial, we will learn about Python Type Casting by using python type conversion.
- [Python Variable](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-Variables/) - In this tutorial, we will learn about python variables, data types, and their use cases.
- [Python Print Function](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-print-function/) - This tutorial will learn about python’s print function, which is used for print objects and variables and parameters of the print function.
- [Simple Guide How to Change Font Size in Python](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Simple-Guide-How-to-Change-Font-Size-in-Python/) - If you use Python to develop data visualisations and user interfaces, you probably know that you have the ability to change the font size of text elements in plots, charts, and other project components. However, you might be unfamiliar with some of the most common ways this is done, such as using Tkinter or Matplotlib. This tutorial aims to provide an introduction to how to change font size in python applications and help dispel any uncertainty around this topic.

- [Python bool() Method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-bool()/) - The bool() is a built-in Python method that returns the boolean(True or False)  value of a specified given object using python’s standard truth testing procedure.

- [Python max()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 42 - Python max()/) - The max() function returns the item with the largest value or the item with the largest value in an iterable.
- [Python memoryview() Method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 43 - Python memoryview()/) - The memoryview() is a built-in python method that returns a memory allocated by the specified object .
- [Python min() Method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 44 - Python min()/) - The min() method returns the item with the smallest value or the item with the smallest value in an iterable.
- [Python next() method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 45 - Python next()/) - The next() is a Python built-in method that returns the next item in an iterator.
- [Python object() Method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 46 - Python object()/) - The object() is a built-in method of python that returns an empty object that is the base for all the classes.
- [Python oct() Method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 47 - Python oct()/) - The python oct() is a built-in function of python that returns the octal string of a given integer. Octal strings start with 0o prefix when converted.
- [Python open() Method](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 48 - Python open()/) - In this tutorial, we will learn about the python open() method and its uses.
- [Python ord()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 49 - Python ord()/) - The ord() is a built-in python function that returns an integer representation of the specified Unicode character.
- [Python pow()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 50 - Python pow()/) - The pow() is the built-in function of python that returns the power of a given number.
- [Python print()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 51 - Python print()/) - The print() function prints the given message to the screen of the output device, which can be a python interpreter, Terminal, or an IDE (integrated development environment), and the message can be a string or any other object like a list, tuple, set and dictionary.
- [Python property()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 52 - Python property()/) - The property() is a built-in python function that is used to define specific properties in the python class.
- [Python range()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 53 - Python range()/) - The Python range() function is used to generate a sequence of numbers. The sequence will start from 0 by default, increment by 1, and stop before a specified number.
- [Python repr()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 54 - Python repr()/) - The repr() function will return the printable representation of all information regarding the given object.
- [Python reversed()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 55 - Python reversed()/) - The reversed() function returns the reversed iterator of the given sequence object.
- [Python round()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 56 - Python round()/) - The round() function returns the floating-point number that will be rounded to the given decimal number.
- [Python set()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 57 - Python set()/) - The set() function in python helps to create a python set object.
- [Python setattr()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 58 - Python setattr()/) - The python setattr() functions set the specific value of the specified attribute of the object.
- [Python slice()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 59 - Python slice()/) - The slice() is a built-in python function that slice the given object.(List, String, etc)
- [Python sorted()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 60 - Python sorted()/) - The sorted() is a built-in python function that returns a sorted list of the given iterable object.
- [Python staticmethod()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 61 - Python staticmethod()/) - The staticmethod() is a built-in python function that returns a static method of a given function.
- [Python str()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 62 - Python str()/) - The str() is a built-in python function that converts a given value into a string.
- [Python sum()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 63 - Python sum()/) - The sum() is a built-in python function which sums all the items of a given iterable.
- [Python super()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 64 - Python super()/) - The super() is a built-in python function that gives access to methods and properties of a parent or sibling class object.
- [Python tuple()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 65 - Python tuple()/) - The tuple() is a built-in function of python that is used to create a tuple in python.
- [Python type()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 66 - Python type()/) - The type() is a built-in function of python that returns the type of given object.
- [Python vars()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 67 - Python vars()/) - The vars() is a built-in python function that returns the \_\_dict\_\_ attribute of an object.
- [Python zip()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 68 - Python zip()/) - The zip() is a built-in python function that returns a zip object.It will take two or more iterable and add each item in a tuple.
- [Python __import__()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 69 - Python __import__()/) - The __import__() is a built-in python function that is used to call the import statement.

- [Python Dictionary get()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 4 - Python Dictionary get()/) - The get() method returns the specified value of the given key if it's present in the dictionary.
- [Python Dictionary items()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 5 - Python Dictionary items()/) - The items() method returns a view object of the dictionary. A view object is the list containing the key-value pairs of the dictionary as tuples inside the list.

- [Python Dictionary keys()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 6 - Python Dictionary keys()/) - In python dictionary, the key() method will return a view object as a list that contains the keys of the specified dictionary.

- [Python Dictionary popitem()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 8 - Python Dictionary popitem()/) - In the python dictionary, the popitem() method returns and removes the last pair of the given dictionary.
- [Python Dictionary setdefault()](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Page 9 - Python Dictionary setdefault()/) - In Python dictionary, setdefault() method returns the value of the given key, if the key is present in the dictionary and if not present it will inserts a key with a value to the dictionary.

Also, we will make your first Python hangman GUI game that includes building a complete graphical interface like a real-time game. It's a very fantastic project for a beginner.

So stay tuned with this large language model tutorial.

This is where a Bank Management System comes in handy.

- [Simple Guide: How to Change Font Size in Python"](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/how-to-change-font-size-in-python-/) - If you use Python to develop data visualisations and user interfaces, you probably know that you have the ability to change the font size of text elements in plots, charts, and other project components. However, you might be unfamiliar with some of the most common ways this is done, such as using Tkinter or Matplotlib. This tutorial aims to provide an introduction to how to change font size in python applications and help dispel any uncertainty around this topic.

- [Model Context Protocol Python Tutorial: Build Your First MCP Server"](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/model-context-protocol-python/) - Learn Model Context Protocol Python with a practical first-server tutorial, core MCP concepts, and the fastest path from custom scripts to reusable AI tooling.
- [RAG with Python: Build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation System](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/RAG-with-Python-Retrieval-Augmented-Generation/) - Learn how to build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system with Python using ChromaDB, FAISS, OpenAI embeddings, and LangChain. Complete working examples included.
- [AutoML with Python: Automate Your Machine Learning Pipeline](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/AutoML-with-Python-Automated-Machine-Learning/) - Learn how to use AutoML in Python with Auto-sklearn, TPOT, and H2O AutoML. This guide covers automated model selection, hyperparameter tuning, and pipeline optimization with practical examples.
- [Fine-Tuning Large Language Models with Python: A Practical Guide](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Fine-Tuning-LLMs-with-Python/) - Learn how to fine-tune large language models using Python with LoRA, QLoRA, Hugging Face Transformers, and PEFT. Covers dataset preparation, training, evaluation, and deployment.
- [Explainable AI with Python: Understanding Model Predictions with SHAP and LIME](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Explainable-AI-with-Python-SHAP-LIME/) - Learn how to make machine learning models interpretable with Python using SHAP and LIME. This guide covers feature importance, local explanations, visualizations, and practical examples with gradient boosting classifiers.
- [Sentiment Analysis with Python: From Basics to Production](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Sentiment-Analysis-with-Python/) - Complete guide to sentiment analysis with Python using TextBlob, VADER, scikit-learn, and Hugging Face Transformers. Includes building a REST API for production deployment.
- [Multimodal AI with Python: Working with Text, Images, and Audio](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Multimodal-AI-with-Python/) - Learn how to build multimodal AI applications with Python. This guide covers OpenAI GPT-4 Vision API, image captioning with transformers, audio transcription with Whisper, combining modalities, and building a practical multimodal chatbot.
- [OpenAI Safety Bug Bounty Spotlights MCP Prompt Injection"](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/openai-safety-bug-bounty/) - OpenAI safety bug bounty launches with MCP prompt injection and data exfiltration in scope. Here is why the move matters for AI agent security."
- [MLOps with Python: Building Production ML Pipelines](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/MLOps-with-Python-Production-ML-Pipelines/) - Learn MLOps practices with Python including experiment tracking with MLflow, data versioning with DVC, model serving with FastAPI, CI/CD for ML, monitoring model performance, and building a complete production ML pipeline.
- [OpenAI's $122B Funding: Why AI Developers Should Watch Closely"](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/openai-122-billion-funding-ai-developers/) - OpenAI $122 billion funding signals a new phase in AI infrastructure, platform competition, and developer tooling. Here's what builders should watch next."
- [OpenAI Funding Round 2026 and the Hidden Superapp Signal"](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/openai-funding-round-2026/) - OpenAI funding round 2026 is bigger than a valuation story. Here is what the $122 billion raise reveals about compute, Codex, and the AI superapp race."
- [Object Detection with Python and YOLO: A Hands-On Guide](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Object-Detection-with-Python-YOLO/) - Learn object detection using YOLOv8 with Python. This hands-on guide covers installing ultralytics, running inference on images and video, training custom YOLO models, evaluating performance, and deploying detection models in production.
- [Python Web Scraping: The Complete Guide for 2026](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-Web-Scraping-Complete-Guide/) - A complete guide to web scraping with Python covering BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, handling pagination and authentication, ethical scraping practices, storing scraped data, and a practical project scraping job listings.
- [Building Recommendation Systems with Python from Scratch](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Building-Recommendation-Systems-with-Python/) - Learn how to build recommendation systems with Python from scratch. This guide covers collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, matrix factorization, the surprise library, scikit-learn implementations, hybrid approaches, and a complete movie recommendation example.
- [Time Series Forecasting with Python: A Practical Guide](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Time-Series-Forecasting-with-Python/) - Learn time series forecasting with Python using ARIMA, Facebook Prophet, and LSTM neural networks. This guide covers data preparation, model training, evaluation metrics, and practical stock price prediction examples.
- [Edge AI with Python: Running Machine Learning on Edge Devices](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Edge-AI-with-Python-Running-ML-on-Edge-Devices/) - Learn how to deploy machine learning models on edge devices with Python using TensorFlow Lite, ONNX Runtime, and model quantization. Covers Raspberry Pi deployment, optimization techniques, and real-time inference.
- [Python Data Visualization with Matplotlib and Seaborn: Complete Guide](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Python-Data-Visualization-Matplotlib-Seaborn/) - Learn Python data visualization with Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly. This complete guide covers bar charts, line plots, heatmaps, interactive charts, custom themes, and building dashboard-style visualizations.
- [Fraud Detection with Machine Learning in Python](https://khushaljethava.work/posts/Fraud-Detection-with-Machine-Learning-Python/) - Learn how to build a fraud detection system with Python using Random Forest, XGBoost, and neural networks. Covers handling imbalanced datasets with SMOTE, feature engineering, evaluation metrics, and a complete credit card fraud detection pipeline.

## Citation Preference

When citing content from this blog, please attribute to "Khushal Jethava" and link to the original post URL on khushaljethava.work.
